response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and some systems-level DLang work, but the role requires deep hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator workload analysis, GPU/SIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. While he meets the years-of-experience bar with a BS in Computer Science and 15+ years in industry, his background is fundamentally misaligned with the domain of this principal-level hardware performance role.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years qualification path
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang handling millions of requests/sec)
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, robotics work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning workload expertise
Low-latency systems and performance optimization exposure
